tasks.title: Consolidate the HX-05 exploration evidence into one governed inventory of global durable state, residue, spec-local evidence, event emitters, and report surfaces

handoffs.summary: Opened HX-05 by adding one governed inventory section covering durable state, derived projections, residue, spec-local evidence, event emitters, and derived report surfaces.
Recorded structural validation after syncing the umbrella queue and landing the opening HX-05 inventory slice.

evidence.summary: Cross-reference, file-existence, and manifest-coherence validation stayed green after HX-05 T-1.1 landed and the umbrella queue advanced to the state-plane wave.

tasks.title: Run the HX-05 governance review on durable-versus-derived-versus-residue classification, serialized families, and deferred ownership before implementation begins

handoffs.summary: Returned PASS-WITH-NOTES; T-1.3 now owns the compatibility matrix for quasi-authoritative current files and readers while the ordered families and deferred ownership cuts remain unchanged.

evidence.summary: HX-05 can proceed to T-1.3 without reopening earlier slices; the remaining concerns are compatibility-boundary work, not a contradiction in the opening state-plane inventory.

tasks.title: Define the HX-05 compatibility boundary for quasi-authoritative current files, event-writer paths, and downstream report consumers

handoffs.summary: Added the HX-05 compatibility matrix for current state projections, residue outputs, event-writer paths, and downstream consumers so the migration stays compatibility-first.
Recorded the passing cross-reference and file-existence validation for the HX-05 compatibility-boundary slice.

evidence.summary: The HX-05 compatibility boundary landed cleanly and Phase 1 can now move to T-2.1 durability-versus-residue coverage.

tasks.title: Write the HX-05 RED contract tests for durable global state, derived projections, residue outputs, and spec-local evidence relocation

handoffs.summary: Added the first explicit HX-05 RED contract slice for shared state-plane classification across durable core, derived current projections, residue outputs, and spec-local evidence relocation.
Recorded the focused RED pytest run proving the state-plane contract exports are still missing from `state.control_plane`.

evidence.summary: The new HX-05 state-plane contract tests fail locally and cleanly, opening T-2.2 implementation without unrelated breakage.

tasks.title: Implement the HX-05 durable-versus-residue contract and classify ambiguous global state files without widening report ownership

handoffs.summary: Implemented the first executable HX-05 state-plane contract so durable state, derived projections, residue, deferred learning-funnel residue, and spec-local spillover now share one classifier.
Recorded the passing focused pytest and structural validation after the new state-plane contract landed in `state.control_plane`.

evidence.summary: The shared HX-05 state-plane contract is now executable and the repo stayed structurally green while the queue advanced to T-2.3.

tasks.title: Relocate spec-local audit and classification artifacts into the spec evidence lane with readable global compatibility shims

handoffs.summary: Moved the canonical `spec-116` audit artifacts under the spec-local evidence lane and added a shared resolver so compatibility readers can still reach the legacy state paths safely.
Recorded the passing focused unit, integration, and structural validation for the `HX-05` spec-local evidence relocation slice.

evidence.summary: Spec-local evidence now resolves canonically from `.ai-engineering/specs/evidence/spec-116/`, the legacy state paths remain readable shims, and the repo stayed green while `HX-05` advanced to T-3.1.

tasks.title: Write failing tests for canonical provider IDs, event kinds, root event fields, task trace fields, and writer-path parity across runtime and hook emitters

handoffs.summary: Added RED coverage for canonical event kinds, legacy Copilot provider normalization, root trace-field parity, and the missing task-trace emitter surface before the event contract cutover begins.
Recorded the expected RED failures that now pin the remaining canonical event-contract and task-trace gaps for `HX-05` T-3.2 and T-3.3.

evidence.summary: The repo now has executable RED coverage proving noncanonical event kinds are still accepted, Copilot provider ids remain unnormalized, and task-trace emitters do not exist yet.

tasks.title: Implement the canonical event contract and route supported writers through the shared vocabulary without adding a second audit log

handoffs.summary: Canonicalized event kinds and provider ids across runtime plus live and template hook writers, and replaced bespoke malformed hook kinds with canonical `ide_hook` warning events.
Recorded the passing focused validation proving the canonical event vocabulary now holds across runtime, active hooks, and telemetry emitters.

evidence.summary: The shared event schema now rejects noncanonical kinds, legacy Copilot ids normalize to `copilot`, and active hook emitters stay parity-locked with runtime root trace fields.

tasks.title: Add authoritative task-trace emission over real work-plane mutations without heuristic task inference

handoffs.summary: Added canonical `emit_task_trace` helpers and wired `write_task_ledger(...)` to append task traces only when authoritative task state or artifact refs actually change.
Recorded the passing focused validation for runtime or hook task-trace parity and task-ledger-triggered append-only emission.

evidence.summary: Task traces now append from authoritative task-ledger mutations with handoff or evidence refs, unchanged ledger writes stay silent, and no generic kernel outcome was heuristically mapped onto a task id.

tasks.title: Run the HX-05 governance review on task traces, audit-chain integrity, and derived-versus-authoritative semantics before scorecard work begins

handoffs.summary: Returned PASS-WITH-NOTES; task traces are now tied to authoritative ledger mutations and the lack of a kernel task-trace callsite remains acceptable until a task-aware kernel seam exists.

evidence.summary: HX-05 can proceed to T-4.1 without reopening earlier slices; the only carry-forward note is that audit-view append failure remains possible after authoritative ledger persistence.

tasks.title: Add derived task scorecards and report reducers for resolution, retry, rework, verification tax, and drift without creating peer authorities

handoffs.summary: Added a derived task scorecard reducer over the active task ledger and canonical framework events, then threaded those metrics through the maintenance report JSON and Markdown views.
Recorded the passing unit and integration validation for the new maintenance-report-derived task scorecard slice.

evidence.summary: Task resolution, retry, rework, verification-tax, and drift metrics are now derived from authoritative state inputs and surfaced only through maintenance-report reducers, not as new peer state files.

tasks.title: Serialize authoritative task writes and derived maintenance-report reads against the framework-events snapshot barrier

handoffs.summary: Moved authoritative task-ledger mutation plus downstream task-trace append behind a shared framework-events snapshot barrier and taught maintenance-report reducers to read the same barrier.
Recorded the focused sequencing RED/green cycle and the passing observability plus maintenance-report validation bundle.

evidence.summary: Authoritative task-ledger writes, task-trace appends, validation-emitted framework events, and maintenance-report reducers now serialize on the same snapshot barrier instead of racing as partially ordered readers and writers.

tasks.title: Run the focused HX-05 verification bundle for state classification, canonical events, task traces, scorecards, and sequencing before strict cutover

handoffs.summary: Recorded the passing HX-05 verification bundle spanning state-plane classification, canonical events, task traces, scorecards, and the snapshot sequencing barrier.

evidence.summary: The Phase 4 state-plane contract is now proven across classification, observability, runtime or hook parity, derived maintenance reducers, and sequencing, clearing HX-05 for strict consumer cutover.

tasks.title: Flip runtime and validation consumers to canonical state-plane paths now that compatibility shims are only fallback artifacts

handoffs.summary: Cut the state-plane resolver and file-existence validator over to canonical spec-local evidence paths so compatibility shims stay readable but stop behaving like authoritative inputs.
Recorded the passing focused verification bundle for canonical resolver behavior, state-plane path parsing, file-existence cutover, and spec-116 lifecycle consumers.

evidence.summary: Runtime and validation consumers now resolve and enforce canonical state-plane paths while leaving the compatibility shim files readable for legacy inspection only.

tasks.title: Document the remaining HX-05 follow-on ownership left intentionally to HX-06, HX-07, and HX-11

handoffs.summary: Added one explicit deferred-follow-on section naming the remaining capability, learning-funnel, and eval/report taxonomy ownership that HX-05 intentionally leaves to HX-06, HX-07, and HX-11.
Recorded the passing structural validation after syncing the deferred-ownership envelope into the HX-05 spec and work-plane artifacts.

evidence.summary: The remaining ownership line for capability projections, learning-funnel lifecycle, and eval/report taxonomy is now explicit in the HX-05 spec instead of being left implicit in non-goals and scattered notes.

tasks.title: Run the final HX-05 proof bundle across state-plane classification, observability, reducers, cutover, and structural validation

handoffs.summary: Recorded the final passing HX-05 proof bundle plus the required structural validators, closing the feature wave end to end.

evidence.summary: HX-05 is now fully proven across state-plane classification, canonical events, authoritative traces, sequencing, reducers, strict canonical readers, and structural integrity checks.
